ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink. FOODON_03311737 (herbal beverage food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Herbal infusions and similar beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Luo Han Guo Longan drink.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional herbal beverage combination.

main_ingredients: Luo Han Guo (monk fruit, Siraitia grosvenorii)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g) of chilled herbal drink, as typically served in a cup or bowl at a Singapore hawker stall or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Longan juice, canned (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy) and Monk Fruit / Luo Han Guo compositional data (FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table); composite estimate for herbal drink preparation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for longan juice/fruit (FDC ID 167762) with published compositional data for Luo Han Guo extract (primarily water, mogroside sweeteners, negligible protein and fat) and accounting for dilution in a typical boiled herbal drink preparation. Rock sugar addition is variable and not assumed in base estimate. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson et al.) — longan fruit and dilute fruit-based herbal drinks analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on: (1) longan fruit GI reported at approximately 50 in the University of Sydney GI Database; (2) significant dilution in herbal drink preparation reduces effective glycemic response; (3) Luo Han Guo (monk fruit) contributes mogroside sweeteners which are non-glycemic and do not raise blood glucose. The composite drink is estimated at GI 45, low category. GL per 300 g serving = (45 × 5.2 × 3) / 100 = 7.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~9 mg per 300 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink is very low in sodium (~9 mg per serving) and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided no added salt or high-sodium ingredients are used in preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Natural sugars from longan fruit and variable rock sugar addition
type2_diabetes.guidance: This herbal drink contains natural sugars from longan (~14.4 g per 300 ml serving if unsweetened) and may have additional rock sugar added; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request unsweetened preparation and consume in moderation, monitoring portion size to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; antioxidant compounds from monk fruit mogrosides
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink is essentially fat-free and contains antioxidant mogrosides from monk fruit, making it a low cardiovascular risk beverage when consumed without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink (罗汉果龙眼饮) is a traditional Chinese herbal cold beverage popular in Singapore and Southern China. It is prepared by boiling dried monk fruit (Luo Han Guo, Siraitia grosvenorii) together with dried longan (龙眼, Dimocarpus longan) in water, sometimes with the addition of rock sugar, and then chilling the resulting liquid. The drink has a naturally sweet, slightly floral flavour with a mild caramel-like undertone from the longan, and a clean, refreshing finish from the monk fruit. It is served cold (冷) and is commonly found at Singapore hawker centres, herbal drink stalls, and hot pot restaurants. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage (~66 kcal per 300 ml serving) with moderate natural sugar content from longan fruit. Monk fruit contributes non-glycemic mogroside sweeteners, which do not raise blood glucose, making the unsweetened version relatively suitable for individuals monitoring sugar intake. The drink is very low in sodium, fat-free, and contains small amounts of potassium and vitamin C from the longan. In traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), Luo Han Guo is valued for its cooling properties and is used to soothe the throat and reduce internal heat. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of added rock sugar versions and prefer unsweetened preparations.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Luo Han Guo Longan herbal drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy longan data and published Luo Han Guo compositional literature, adjusted for typical herbal drink dilution. GI is an analogous estimate based on longan fruit GI and the non-glycemic nature of monk fruit mogrosides. Confidence is medium.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (longan juice/fruit, FDC ID 167762) and FAO/INFOODS SEA compositional data for Luo Han Guo, adjusted for typical herbal drink preparation dilution. Rock sugar is not assumed in the base nutrient estimate; actual sugar content will be higher if rock sugar is added. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage type. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based herbal beverage with negligible fat content. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are confirmed zero per source data for dilute longan-based beverages. trans_fat_g is naturally zero as no hydrogenated fats are present. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage. GI set to 45 (low) as an analogous estimate based on longan fruit GI (~50, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward for dilution and the non-glycemic mogroside contribution of monk fruit. GL per 300 g serving calculated as (45 × 5.2 × 3.0) / 100 = 7.0.
